seoTitle: How to Remove Duplicates in Excel 365 Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Clean repeated rows out of your Excel 365 spreadsheet using conditional formatting to spot duplicates, the Remove Duplicates tool, or UNIQUE().
intro: Messy customer lists and long tracking sheets often end up with repeated rows that skew your totals. Excel 365 gives you a few ways to handle this: visually flag duplicates first with conditional formatting, permanently strip them out with the built-in removal tool, or pull a clean list with the non-destructive UNIQUE function.

steps.heading: Highlight duplicates visually first
steps.body: Select your data column, go to Conditional Formatting on the Home tab, and choose Duplicate Values to color-code repeated entries before deleting anything.

steps.heading: Use the Remove Duplicates tool
steps.body: Click inside your data range, go to the Data tab, and select Remove Duplicates from the Data Tools section to permanently strip out repeated rows.

steps.heading: Choose which columns define a duplicate
steps.body: Keep the header checkbox checked, then decide whether to base duplicates on a single column like email, or match across the entire row.

steps.heading: Review the cleanup summary
steps.body: Confirm the removal and check the summary popup that tells you exactly how many duplicate rows were deleted.

steps.heading: Try the non-destructive UNIQUE formula
steps.body: In an empty cell, type the UNIQUE function referencing your full table range to spill out a separate, self-updating list of only the unique records.

faq.question: Will removing duplicates delete my original data permanently?
faq.answer: Yes, the Remove Duplicates tool permanently deletes rows, so it's worth saving a backup copy of your file before running it.

faq.question: What's the difference between Remove Duplicates and UNIQUE?
faq.answer: Remove Duplicates permanently deletes rows in place, while UNIQUE creates a separate, non-destructive list that leaves your original data untouched.

faq.question: Can I remove duplicates based on just one column?
faq.answer: Yes, when the Remove Duplicates dialog opens, you can uncheck columns you don't want considered, such as basing duplicates only on an email address column.

faq.question: How do I preview duplicates before deleting anything?
faq.answer: Use Conditional Formatting's Duplicate Values rule first to visually highlight repeated rows so you can review them before running a permanent removal.
